contents:
# Department of the Treasury’s Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) imposed sanctions on former Democratic Republic of the Congo President Joseph Kabange Kabila in Washington,...

## Overview
On April 30, 2026, the U.S. Department of the Treasury’s Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) imposed sanctions on former Democratic Republic of the Congo President **Joseph Kabange Kabila** in Washington, D.C., for his role in supporting the Rwanda-backed M23 armed group and the Congo River Alliance (AFC), which seek to topple the DRC government.[1] M23, a U.S.- and UN-designated group, has captured territories including Goma and Bukavu in eastern DRC with RDF support, sanctioned by OFAC on March 2, 2026.[1] The action blocks all U.S.-based property and interests of Kabila, with entities 50% or more owned by him also blocked.[1] No Treasury or OFAC designations, enforcement actions, or financial intelligence announcements occurred in the past hour as of 8:06 PM UTC.[1][2][3][4][5]
